Title: Display Carbon Footprint Metrics for Cloud Services
Preconditions: User is logged in to the app and has connected at least one cloud service (e.g., Google Drive).
Test Data: Sample data for cloud storage usage (e.g., 5GB stored, 10 uploads/downloads in the last month).
Test Steps:
1. User opens the dashboard.
2. User navigates to the "Cloud Services" section.
3. System displays a summary of carbon emissions associated with cloud storage and data transfers.
Expected Result:
The dashboard shows accurate and real-time carbon footprint metrics for cloud services, including a breakdown of emissions per activity (e.g., storage, transfers).

Title: Award Badge for Reducing Carbon Footprint by 10%
Preconditions: User has reduced their carbon footprint by at least 10% compared to the previous week.
Test Data: Sample data showing a 10% reduction in carbon emissions from the baseline.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the app.
2. System calculates the user’s carbon footprint reduction over the last week.
3. User completes a week with a 10% reduction.
4. System awards a badge for the achievement.
Expected Result:
The app displays a new badge in the user’s profile, labeled "Eco-Saver," and shows a congratulatory message.
--

3. User views the top 5 users in the leaderboard.

The leaderboard displays the top 5 users with the highest percentage of carbon footprint reduction, along with their usernames and reduction percentages.

The app successfully connects to Google Drive, retrieves storage and activity data, and starts tracking carbon emissions associated with cloud usage.
